1	import AppKit
2	
3	/// `NSTableViewDataSource` + `NSTableViewDelegate` for the transcript table.
4	///
5	/// Single source of truth: `blocks: [Block]`. Layout is treated as a **pure
6	/// derivation** of `(block, width)` — `layoutCache` is a memo, not a parallel
7	/// truth. There is no `rows` mirror, no sync invariant between data and
8	/// layout, no diff anywhere.
9	///
10	/// ### Mutation paths
11	///
12	/// - **`apply(_:scroll:)`** — sync. Layouts compute lazily on `heightOfRow`
13	///   queries. Used for incremental updates (live messages, single removals,
14	///   tool-result updates).
15	/// - **`applyInBackground(_:scroll:)`** — layouts for the inserted blocks
16	///   compute on a detached `Task`, then a main hop installs them and runs
17	///   the structural change in one shot. Used by `Controller.loadInitial`'s
18	///   Phase 2 (large prepend after the viewport batch is already visible).
19	///
20	/// Both paths run their structural change inside `withScrollAdjustment`,
21	/// which interprets `ScrollState`:
22	/// - `.none` — no scroll work.
23	/// - `.top(id)` / `.bottom(id)` — direct scroll-to-position after the change.
24	/// - `.saveVisible(side)` — capture an anchor row's screen position before,
25	///   compensate scroll origin after so the row stays visually fixed across
26	///   the structural change. Same trick as Telegram's `saveScrollState` in
27	///   `TableView.layoutItems()`.
28	///
29	/// ### Width change (resize)
30	///
31	/// `layoutCache` is keyed by `(id, width)`. When the table width changes,
32	/// existing entries become misses and lazy-recompute. `tableFrameDidChange`
33	/// invalidates rows; live resize bounds work to visible rows;
34	/// `refillLayoutCache` (post-resize) reuses the same async pipeline
35	/// (`precomputeLayoutsInBackground` → `cacheLayouts` → `noteHeightOfRows`
36	/// under `.saveVisible(.visualTop)`).
37	///
38	/// ### Concurrency
39	///
40	/// Everything is `@MainActor`. Two distinct off-main lifecycles, kept
41	/// apart on purpose because their AppKit semantics differ:
42	///
43	/// - **`cacheRefillTask`** — `tableFrameDidChange` post-resize refill.
44	///   `numberOfRows` doesn't change; the only effect is to populate
45	///   `layoutCache` at the new width and `noteHeightOfRows` the rows whose
46	///   heights moved. Superseded only by the next `refillLayoutCache`. Loss is
47	///   CPU only — `heightOfRow` lazy-recomputes.
48	///
49	/// - **`applyInBackground`'s detached task** — row-mutation precompute.
50	///   Fire-and-forget, *not* tracked by a field, *not* cancellable: the
51	///   `insertRows` it carries is `dataSource`-changing critical work and
52	///   has to land. `Change.insert` resolves its anchor by id at apply-time,
53	///   so landing is robust against inflight `apply`s in between.
54	///
55	/// Cache anti-poison sits inside `cacheLayouts`: a write skips entries
56	/// already fresh at the same width, so an inflight task hopping in *after*
57	/// `apply .update`/`.remove` evicted and lazy-refilled an entry can't
58	/// overwrite the authoritative fresh layout with its older snapshot.
59	///
60	/// On top of that, a `mutationCounter` snapshot lets the refill task
61	/// drop its entire onMain block (including `noteHeightOfRows` and
62	/// `saveVisible`) when an `apply` ran during the task — running
63	/// `saveVisible` against stale AppKit heights (deferred re-query) would
64	/// otherwise jitter the anchor row.
65	@MainActor
66	final class Transcript2Coordinator: NSObject, NSTableViewDataSource, NSTableViewDelegate {
67	    weak var tableView: NSTableView? {
68	        didSet {
69	            // If `apply` was called before the table was attached, blocks
70	            // already exist; the freshly-attached table starts at
71	            // `numberOfRows = 0` until reloaded.
72	            if let table = tableView, oldValue !== tableView, !blocks.isEmpty {
73	                table.reloadData()
74	            }
75	        }
76	    }
77	
78	    /// Notifies the controller after every successful mutation so SwiftUI
79	    /// observers on `blockCount` see the new value.
80	    var onBlockCountChanged: ((Int) -> Void)?
81	
82	    /// Set by `Transcript2Controller` to forward chevron taps to the
83	    /// SwiftUI-owned sheet binding. The cell's mouseDown handler resolves
84	    /// the chevron hit, looks up the source `Block.Kind.userBubble(text:)`,
85	    /// and fires this — keeping the cross-layer signal narrow (one block
86	    /// id + the original text) so neither side reaches into the other's
87	    /// internals.
88	    var onUserBubbleSheetRequested: ((UUID, String) -> Void)?
89	
90	    /// Cross-row text selection. Owns the selection dict; reads back into
91	    /// us through the helpers below (`block(atRow:)`, `textLayout(atRow:)`,
92	    /// `attributedString(forBlockId:)`, `markCellNeedsDisplay(blockId:)`).
93	    let selection: Transcript2SelectionCoordinator
94	
95	    /// Async-filled per-block side data. Currently backs syntax tokens
96	    /// for code blocks; future highlight-shaped derivatives (diff hunks,
97	    /// inline annotations) will share the same storage by adding scopes.
98	    /// `apply` lifecycle wires `schedule` / `drop` calls; `onDidFill`
99	    /// drives a single-row reload after tokens land.
100	    let highlightStorage: Transcript2HighlightStorage
101	
102	    init(syntaxEngine: SyntaxHighlightEngine? = nil) {
103	        self.selection = Transcript2SelectionCoordinator()
104	        self.highlightStorage = Transcript2HighlightStorage(engine: syntaxEngine)
105	        super.init()
106	        self.selection.transcript = self
107	        self.highlightStorage.onDidFill = { [weak self] id in
108	            self?.handleHighlightDidFill(blockId: id)
109	        }
110	    }
111	
112	    /// Late-bind a syntax engine. Hosts that read `\.syntaxEngine` from
113	    /// SwiftUI environment hop here after `body` resolves the value.
114	    /// On `nil → engine` transition, every currently-installed block is
115	    /// re-scheduled so cold-loaded code blocks pick up tokens; passing
116	    /// the same engine again is harmless (the per-block generation guard
117	    /// dedupes redundant in-flight tasks).
118	    func attachSyntaxEngine(_ engine: SyntaxHighlightEngine?) {
119	        let wasAttached = highlightStorage.hasEngine
120	        highlightStorage.setEngine(engine)
121	        guard !wasAttached, engine != nil else { return }
122	        for block in blocks { highlightStorage.schedule(block) }
123	    }
124	
125	    private var blocks: [Block] = []
126	
127	    /// Memo of `(block, width) -> RowLayout`. Keyed by id so updates and
128	    /// removes can evict in O(1). The `width` field invalidates the entry
129	    /// when the table width changes — lookups at a different width treat the
130	    /// entry as a miss and overwrite it on recompute, so the cache never
131	    /// holds layouts at multiple widths simultaneously.
132	    private var layoutCache: [UUID: CachedLayout] = [:]
133	
134	    private struct CachedLayout {
135	        let width: CGFloat
136	        let layout: RowLayout
137	    }
138	
139	    /// Tracks the `tableFrameDidChange` post-resize layout refill task.
140	    /// Superseded only by the next `refillLayoutCache`. `applyInBackground`'s
141	    /// detached task is intentionally *not* stored here — it's
142	    /// fire-and-forget so an in-flight row-mutation can't be interrupted
143	    /// by an unrelated cancel path.
144	    private var cacheRefillTask: Task<Void, Never>?
145	
146	    /// Bumped on every `apply`. The refill task captures it at start; if
147	    /// it drifts during the task run, the entire onMain block (cache
148	    /// writes, `noteHeightOfRows`, `saveVisible`) is dropped. Reason: the
149	    /// `saveVisible` anchor math relies on AppKit re-querying heights for
150	    /// rows we just `noteHeightOfRows`'d, but that re-query is deferred
151	    /// to the next layout pass — so running `applyAnchor` immediately
152	    /// after compensates against stale internal heights and the row
153	    /// visually jumps when AppKit eventually catches up. Skipping refill
154	    /// in this case is harmless: `apply`'s own scroll already settled the
155	    /// post-mutation state, and `heightOfRow` lazy-fills missing layouts
156	    /// on demand. `applyInBackground` doesn't bump because its own
157	    /// row-mutation is the change-event for its own scroll handling.
158	    private var mutationCounter: UInt64 = 0
159	
160	    // MARK: - Read-only snapshot
161	
162	    var blockIds: [UUID] { blocks.map(\.id) }
163	
164	    /// Width that rows are laid out at — clamped to
165	    /// `BlockStyle.[min,max]LayoutWidth`. Driven by the single column's
166	    /// `.autoresizingMask` (which tracks the table width). Returns 0 if
167	    /// the table isn't attached or hasn't been sized yet.
168	    ///
169	    /// Clamping here is the single source of truth: `makeLayout` sees the
170	    /// clamped width, `layoutCache` keys on it, and `CenteredRowView` /
171	    /// `Transcript2SelectionCoordinator` both consume `BlockStyle`'s
172	    /// helpers to stay in sync. Window resizes that don't cross the
173	    /// clamp boundary land on the same cache entry — no relayout.
174	    var layoutWidth: CGFloat {
175	        guard let raw = tableView?.tableColumns.first?.width, raw > 0 else { return 0 }
176	        return BlockStyle.clampedLayoutWidth(forRowWidth: raw)
177	    }
178	
179	    /// Last `layoutWidth` we processed in `tableFrameDidChange`. Used to
180	    /// short-circuit notifications whose underlying column-width change
181	    /// didn't move the clamped value (resize within the >max band).
182	    /// Sentinel `-1` will not match any real width on first run.
183	    private var lastLayoutWidth: CGFloat = -1
184	
185	    /// Visible-region height of the enclosing scroll view. Returns 0 if
186	    /// no scroll view is attached.
187	    var viewportHeight: CGFloat {
188	        tableView?.enclosingScrollView?.contentView.bounds.height ?? 0
189	    }
190	
191	    private var transcriptScrollView: Transcript2ScrollView? {
192	        tableView?.enclosingScrollView as? Transcript2ScrollView
193	    }
194	
195	    /// Forwarders for `Transcript2ScrollView`'s scroller-hidden refcount.
196	    /// Silently no-op when no scroll view is attached — push/pop balance
197	    /// holds because both will no-op together.
198	    func pushScrollerHidden() { transcriptScrollView?.pushScrollerHidden() }
199	    func popScrollerHidden()  { transcriptScrollView?.popScrollerHidden() }
200	
201	    // MARK: - Mutation: sync
202	
203	    func apply(_ changes: [Transcript2Controller.Change],
204	               scroll: Transcript2Controller.ScrollState = .none) {
205	        // Bump so any inflight `cacheRefillTask` discards its onMain on
206	        // hop. We don't cancel here: the counter is the actual guard, and
207	        // `cacheRefillTask` polices its own lifetime via the next
208	        // `refillLayoutCache`. Discarding refill in this window matters
209	        // because its `saveVisible` would compensate against stale AppKit
210	        // heights (deferred re-query) — running on top of `apply`'s own
211	        // settled scroll would jitter the anchor row.
212	        mutationCounter &+= 1
213	
214	        let table = tableView
215	        if let table {
216	            withScrollAdjustment(scroll, in: table) {
217	                table.beginUpdates()
218	                for change in changes {
219	                    applyStructuralChange(change, in: table)
220	                }
221	                table.endUpdates()
222	            }
223	        } else {
224	            // Table not attached. Just mutate `blocks`; future attach will
225	            // `reloadData()`. Scroll state is meaningless without a table.
226	            for change in changes {
227	                applyStructuralChange(change, in: nil)
228	            }
229	        }
230	
231	        onBlockCountChanged?(blocks.count)
232	    }
233	
234	    // MARK: - Mutation: off-main (Phase 2 of loadInitial, future use cases)
235	
236	    /// Layouts for the inserted blocks compute on a detached task; a single
237	    /// main hop installs them and runs the structural changes under
238	    /// `scroll`.
239	    ///
240	    /// **Fire-and-forget.** The task is not tracked and not cancellable:
241	    /// row-mutation is `dataSource` critical-path work that must land.
242	    /// `Change.insert`'s id-based anchor resolves at apply-time, so
243	    /// landing stays correct across any `apply`s that ran in between.
244	    /// Layout entries enter the cache only on width match; a drifted
245	    /// width keeps the row-mutation but skips the cache write
246	    /// (`heightOfRow` lazy-recomputes at the new width).
247	    ///
248	    /// `completion` fires on main exactly once, in every outcome
249	    /// (succeeded, table-detached, zero-width). Callers use it to balance
250	    /// paired lifecycle work (e.g. scroller push/pop) that must survive
251	    /// the async hop.
252	    func applyInBackground(_ changes: [Transcript2Controller.Change],
253	                           scroll: Transcript2Controller.ScrollState,
254	                           completion: @MainActor @escaping () -> Void = {}) {
255	        guard tableView != nil else { completion(); return }
256	        let width = layoutWidth
257	        guard width > 0 else { completion(); return }
258	
259	        // Only `.insert` carries new blocks; `.remove` / `.update` either
260	        // don't add layouts or evict them. `.update`'s replacement layout
261	        // is computed lazily by `applyStructuralChange` after the main hop.
262	        let toCompute: [Block] = changes.flatMap { change -> [Block] in
263	            if case .insert(_, let blocks) = change { return blocks }
264	            return []
265	        }
266	
267	        // Snapshot highlight tokens on MainActor before detaching — the
268	        // off-main loop reads per-block tokens from this dict instead of
269	        // hopping back to the actor mid-iteration. New tokens that land
270	        // between this snapshot and the main hop are picked up by the
271	        // `onDidFill` reload path independently.
272	        let tokensSnapshot = highlightStorage.snapshot()
273	
274	        Task.detached(priority: .userInitiated) { [weak self] in
275	            var entries: [(UUID, RowLayout)] = []
276	            entries.reserveCapacity(toCompute.count)
277	            for block in toCompute {
278	                entries.append((block.id, Self.makeLayout(
279	                    for: block, width: width, tokens: tokensSnapshot)))
280	            }
281	            await MainActor.run { [entries] in
282	                defer { completion() }
283	                guard let self, let table = self.tableView else { return }
284	                self.withScrollAdjustment(scroll, in: table) {
285	                    if self.layoutWidth == width {
286	                        self.cacheLayouts(entries, width: width)
287	                    }
288	                    table.beginUpdates()
289	                    for change in changes {
290	                        self.applyStructuralChange(change, in: table)
291	                    }
292	                    table.endUpdates()
293	                }
294	                self.onBlockCountChanged?(self.blocks.count)
295	            }
296	        }
297	    }
298	
299	    // MARK: - Structural change (mechanical, no scroll, no scheduling)
300	
301	    private func applyStructuralChange(_ change: Transcript2Controller.Change,
302	                                       in table: NSTableView?) {
303	        switch change {
304	        case .insert(let after, let new):
305	            guard !new.isEmpty else { return }
306	            let idx: Int
307	            if let after {
308	                guard let i = blocks.firstIndex(where: { $0.id == after }) else { return }
309	                idx = i + 1
310	            } else {
311	                idx = 0
312	            }
313	            blocks.insert(contentsOf: new, at: idx)
314	            for block in new { highlightStorage.schedule(block) }
315	            table?.insertRows(at: IndexSet(idx ..< idx + new.count),
316	                              withAnimation: [.effectFade])
317	
318	        case .remove(let ids):
319	            let idSet = Set(ids)
320	            var indexes = IndexSet()
321	            for (i, b) in blocks.enumerated() where idSet.contains(b.id) {
322	                indexes.insert(i)
323	            }
324	            guard !indexes.isEmpty else { return }
325	            for i in indexes.reversed() { blocks.remove(at: i) }
326	            for id in idSet {
327	                removeCachedLayout(for: id)
328	                selection.dropEntry(blockId: id)
329	                highlightStorage.drop(blockId: id)
330	            }
331	            table?.removeRows(at: indexes, withAnimation: [.effectFade])
332	
333	        case .update(let id, let kind):
334	            guard let i = blocks.firstIndex(where: { $0.id == id }) else { return }
335	            let updated = Block(id: id, kind: kind)
336	            blocks[i] = updated
337	            removeCachedLayout(for: id)
338	            // Drop stale highlight tokens (the new kind may carry a
339	            // different code or language, or no highlight at all). The
340	            // generation guard inside `drop` also invalidates any
341	            // in-flight task for the previous content, then `schedule`
342	            // kicks the new pass for the replacement kind.
343	            highlightStorage.drop(blockId: id)
344	            highlightStorage.schedule(updated)
345	            // Content replacement invalidates the prior selection range
346	            // (offsets no longer index into the same string). Drop now so
347	            // the upcoming `reloadData(forRowIndexes:)` runs viewFor with
348	            // a clean empty selection on the recycled cell.
349	            selection.dropEntry(blockId: id)
350	            let idx = IndexSet(integer: i)
351	            table?.reloadData(forRowIndexes: idx,
352	                              columnIndexes: IndexSet(integer: 0))
353	            table?.noteHeightOfRows(withIndexesChanged: idx)
354	        }
355	    }
356	
357	    // MARK: - Highlight tokens fill-in
358	
359	    /// Called by `highlightStorage` after async tokens land for `blockId`.
360	    /// Evicts the stale (plain) `RowLayout` and reloads the single row.
361	    /// Skips `noteHeightOfRows` because token fill changes only color,
362	    /// not glyph metrics — a re-layout pass would be a wasted query.
363	    private func handleHighlightDidFill(blockId: UUID) {
364	        guard let table = tableView,
365	              let row = blocks.firstIndex(where: { $0.id == blockId })
366	        else { return }
367	        removeCachedLayout(for: blockId)
368	        table.reloadData(forRowIndexes: IndexSet(integer: row),
369	                         columnIndexes: IndexSet(integer: 0))
370	    }
371	
372	    // MARK: - Scroll adjustment
373	
374	    /// Wraps a structural-change closure with the requested scroll behavior.
375	    /// `.saveVisible` disables implicit animations so the height/insert
376	    /// transition doesn't race with the scroll-origin compensation.
377	    private func withScrollAdjustment(_ scroll: Transcript2Controller.ScrollState,
378	                                      in tableView: NSTableView,
379	                                      body: () -> Void) {
380	        switch scroll {
381	        case .none:
382	            body()
383	        case .top(let id):
384	            body()
385	            scrollRowToTop(id: id, in: tableView)
386	        case .bottom(let id):
387	            body()
388	            scrollRowToBottom(id: id, in: tableView)
389	        case .saveVisible(let side):
390	            let anchor = captureAnchor(side: side, in: tableView)
391	            // Disable both NSAnimationContext (row-height transition) and
392	            // CATransaction (layer-backed ClipView's bounds.origin animation
393	            // from `scroll(to:)`) so they don't race.
394	            NSAnimationContext.beginGrouping()
395	            NSAnimationContext.current.duration = 0
396	            NSAnimationContext.current.allowsImplicitAnimation = false
397	            CATransaction.begin()
398	            CATransaction.setDisableActions(true)
399	            body()
400	            if let anchor { applyAnchor(anchor, in: tableView) }
401	            CATransaction.commit()
402	            NSAnimationContext.endGrouping()
403	        }
404	    }
405	
406	    private struct ScrollAnchor {
407	        let blockId: UUID
408	        /// `rect.origin.y` for `.visualTop`, `rect.maxY` for `.visualBottom`.
409	        let oldRefY: CGFloat
410	        let oldScrollY: CGFloat
411	        let side: Transcript2Controller.ScrollState.Side
412	    }
413	
414	    private func captureAnchor(side: Transcript2Controller.ScrollState.Side,
415	                               in tableView: NSTableView) -> ScrollAnchor? {
416	        guard let scrollView = tableView.enclosingScrollView else { return nil }
417	        let visible = tableView.rows(in: tableView.visibleRect)
418	        guard visible.location != NSNotFound, visible.length > 0 else { return nil }
419	
420	        // NSTableView is flipped (default): smallest visible row index = top
421	        // of viewport; largest = bottom.
422	        let anchorRow: Int
423	        switch side {
424	        case .visualTop:
425	            anchorRow = visible.location
426	        case .visualBottom:
427	            anchorRow = visible.location + visible.length - 1
428	        }
429	        guard blocks.indices.contains(anchorRow) else { return nil }
430	        let rect = tableView.rect(ofRow: anchorRow)
431	        let refY: CGFloat = (side == .visualTop) ? rect.origin.y : rect.maxY
432	        return ScrollAnchor(
433	            blockId: blocks[anchorRow].id,
434	            oldRefY: refY,
435	            oldScrollY: scrollView.contentView.bounds.origin.y,
436	            side: side)
437	    }
438	
439	    private func applyAnchor(_ anchor: ScrollAnchor, in tableView: NSTableView) {
440	        guard let scrollView = tableView.enclosingScrollView else { return }
441	        guard let newRow = blocks.firstIndex(where: { $0.id == anchor.blockId }) else {
442	            return
443	        }
444	        let newRect = tableView.rect(ofRow: newRow)
445	        let newRefY: CGFloat = (anchor.side == .visualTop) ? newRect.origin.y : newRect.maxY
446	        let delta = newRefY - anchor.oldRefY
447	        if abs(delta) > 0.5 {
448	            scrollView.contentView.scroll(
449	                to: NSPoint(x: scrollView.contentView.bounds.origin.x,
450	                            y: anchor.oldScrollY + delta))
451	        }
452	    }
453	
454	    private func scrollRowToTop(id: UUID, in tableView: NSTableView) {
455	        guard let row = blocks.firstIndex(where: { $0.id == id }),
456	              let scrollView = tableView.enclosingScrollView else { return }
457	        let target = tableView.rect(ofRow: row).origin.y
458	        scrollView.contentView.scroll(
459	            to: NSPoint(x: scrollView.contentView.bounds.origin.x, y: target))
460	    }
461	
462	    private func scrollRowToBottom(id: UUID, in tableView: NSTableView) {
463	        guard let row = blocks.firstIndex(where: { $0.id == id }),
464	              let scrollView = tableView.enclosingScrollView else { return }
465	        let rect = tableView.rect(ofRow: row)
466	        let viewportH = scrollView.contentView.bounds.height
467	        let target = max(0, rect.maxY - viewportH)
468	        scrollView.contentView.scroll(
469	            to: NSPoint(x: scrollView.contentView.bounds.origin.x, y: target))
470	    }
471	
472	    // MARK: - Layout cache
473	
474	    /// All access to `layoutCache` goes through these helpers and the lazy
475	    /// `layout(for:width:)` path below. Direct subscripting from elsewhere
476	    /// is banned by convention so the width invariant and the "evict on
477	    /// input change" discipline have a single audit point.
478	
479	    /// Writes precomputed layouts into the cache. Skips an entry if a
480	    /// fresh layout at the same width is already present —— that means the
481	    /// sync `apply` path has run `removeCachedLayout` + a layout pass
482	    /// since this batch was computed, and the lazy re-fill it triggered
483	    /// is the authoritative entry. Overwriting it with our (older
484	    /// snapshot's) layout would poison the cache.
485	    private func cacheLayouts(_ entries: [(UUID, RowLayout)], width: CGFloat) {
486	        for (id, layout) in entries {
487	            if layoutCache[id]?.width == width { continue }
488	            layoutCache[id] = CachedLayout(width: width, layout: layout)
489	        }
490	    }
491	
492	    private func removeCachedLayout(for id: UUID) {
493	        layoutCache.removeValue(forKey: id)
494	    }
495	
496	    private func indexesNeedingLayoutRefresh(at width: CGFloat) -> [Int] {
497	        blocks.indices.filter { layoutCache[blocks[$0].id]?.width != width }
498	    }
499	
500	    // MARK: - Lazy layout (heightOfRow / viewFor)
501	
502	    private func layout(for block: Block, width: CGFloat) -> RowLayout {
503	        if let c = layoutCache[block.id], c.width == width {
504	            return c.layout
505	        }
506	        let layout = Self.makeLayout(
507	            for: block, width: width,
508	            tokens: highlightStorage.snapshot())
509	        layoutCache[block.id] = CachedLayout(width: width, layout: layout)
510	        return layout
511	    }
512	
513	    /// Pure: `(block, width, tokens) -> RowLayout`. `nonisolated static`
514	    /// so the background prefetch task can call it off-MainActor.
515	    /// `tokens` is a snapshot of `Transcript2HighlightStorage` taken on
516	    /// MainActor before the detached task starts; passing the snapshot in
517	    /// keeps the per-block lookup actor-free during the off-main loop.
518	    /// Defaults to empty so call sites that genuinely don't want
519	    /// highlight (e.g. `Transcript2Controller.sliceForViewport`'s
520	    /// height-only probe) can omit the parameter.
521	    nonisolated static func makeLayout(
522	        for block: Block, width: CGFloat,
523	        tokens: [Transcript2HighlightKey: [SyntaxToken]] = [:]
524	    ) -> RowLayout {
525	        let contentWidth = max(0, width - 2 * BlockStyle.blockHorizontalPadding)
526	        switch block.kind {
527	        case .heading(let level, let inlines):
528	            return .text(TextLayout.make(
529	                attributed: BlockStyle.headingAttributed(level: level, inlines: inlines),
530	                maxWidth: contentWidth))
531	        case .paragraph(let inlines):
532	            return .text(TextLayout.make(
533	                attributed: BlockStyle.paragraphAttributed(inlines: inlines),
534	                maxWidth: contentWidth))
535	        case .image(let image):
536	            return .image(ImageLayout.make(
537	                image: image,
538	                maxWidth: contentWidth,
539	                maxHeight: BlockStyle.imageMaxHeight))
540	        case .list(let listBlock):
541	            return .list(ListLayout.make(block: listBlock, maxWidth: contentWidth))
542	        case .table(let tableBlock):
543	            return .table(TableLayout.make(block: tableBlock, maxWidth: contentWidth))
544	        case .codeBlock(let language, let code):
545	            let codeTokens = tokens[
546	                Transcript2HighlightKey(blockId: block.id, scope: .codeBlock)]
547	            return .codeBlock(CodeBlockLayout.make(
548	                code: code, language: language,
549	                tokens: codeTokens, maxWidth: contentWidth))
550	        case .blockquote(let inlines):
551	            return .blockquote(BlockquoteLayout.make(inlines: inlines, maxWidth: contentWidth))
552	        case .thematicBreak:
553	            return .thematicBreak(ThematicBreakLayout.make(maxWidth: contentWidth))
554	        case .userBubble(let text):
555	            return .userBubble(UserBubbleLayout.make(text: text, maxWidth: contentWidth))
556	        }
557	    }
558	
559	    // MARK: - User bubble sheet
560	
561	    /// Forwards a chevron click on the user bubble at `id` to the SwiftUI
562	    /// sheet binding (via `onUserBubbleSheetRequested`). No `.update` path
563	    /// — fold state is absent from the layout; the sheet is the place to
564	    /// read the full message. No-op if `id` is unknown or doesn't point
565	    /// at a `userBubble`.
566	    func requestUserBubbleSheet(id: UUID) {
567	        guard let i = blocks.firstIndex(where: { $0.id == id }),
568	              case .userBubble(let text) = blocks[i].kind
569	        else { return }
570	        onUserBubbleSheetRequested?(id, text)
571	    }
572	
573	    // MARK: - Width-change driven invalidation
574	
575	    @objc func tableFrameDidChange(_ note: Notification) {
576	        guard let tableView, !blocks.isEmpty else { return }
577	        // Resizes inside the >max clamp band leave `layoutWidth` unchanged.
578	        // `CenteredRowView.layout()` still re-runs (driven by NSTableView's
579	        // tile pass) and repositions the cell horizontally, but no row needs
580	        // its layout invalidated — so skip the reload/noteHeightOfRows pair.
581	        let width = layoutWidth
582	        if width == lastLayoutWidth { return }
583	        lastLayoutWidth = width
584	        if tableView.inLiveResize {
585	            // Bounded per-frame layout work: only invalidate visible rows.
586	            // Off-screen rows keep their stale heights and stale cached
587	            // layouts — invisible to the user and repaired by the
588	            // post-resize background prefetch.
589	            let visible = tableView.rows(in: tableView.visibleRect)
590	            guard visible.location != NSNotFound, visible.length > 0 else { return }
591	            invalidate(rows: IndexSet(visible.location ..< visible.location + visible.length),
592	                       in: tableView)
593	        } else {
594	            // Outside live resize, frame changes are programmatic / one-off
595	            // (initial layout, window animation). Invalidate everything;
596	            // AppKit re-queries lazily on next layout pass.
597	            invalidate(rows: IndexSet(0 ..< blocks.count), in: tableView)
598	        }
599	    }
600	
601	    /// `reloadData(forRowIndexes:)` re-runs `viewFor` so the cell picks up
602	    /// the layout at the current width; `noteHeightOfRows` tells AppKit to
603	    /// re-query `heightOfRow` so cell frames resize. Both are needed —
604	    /// dropping `reloadData(forRowIndexes:)` leaves visible cells holding
605	    /// the old `RowLayout` (drawn at old width) inside a newly-resized
606	    /// frame, so glyphs land at the wrong x positions during a live resize.
607	    ///
608	    /// The no-animation grouping is the live-resize fix: by default
609	    /// `noteHeightOfRows` repositions rows below via an implicit
610	    /// NSAnimationContext / CATransaction animation, while cell-internal
611	    /// redraw is synchronous (`needsDisplay = true` in `layout` setter).
612	    /// During fast resize the cell already paints at the new height while
613	    /// the row below is still mid-animation at its old y — visually the
614	    /// rows overlap. Zeroing duration and disabling layer actions makes
615	    /// row repositioning land in the same display cycle as the redraw.
616	    /// Mirrors Telegram's `TableView.layoutIfNeeded(with:oldWidth:)` →
617	    /// `noteHeightOfRow(_:false)` path, which does the same suppression.
618	    private func invalidate(rows indexes: IndexSet, in tableView: NSTableView) {
619	        guard !indexes.isEmpty else { return }
620	        NSAnimationContext.beginGrouping()
621	        NSAnimationContext.current.duration = 0
622	        NSAnimationContext.current.allowsImplicitAnimation = false
623	        CATransaction.begin()
624	        CATransaction.setDisableActions(true)
625	        tableView.beginUpdates()
626	        tableView.reloadData(forRowIndexes: indexes,
627	                             columnIndexes: IndexSet(integer: 0))
628	        tableView.noteHeightOfRows(withIndexesChanged: indexes)
629	        tableView.endUpdates()
630	        CATransaction.commit()
631	        NSAnimationContext.endGrouping()
632	    }
633	
634	    // MARK: - Background prefetch (post-live-resize)
635	
636	    func refillLayoutCache() {
637	        guard let tableView else { return }
638	        let width = layoutWidth
639	        guard width > 0 else { return }
640	        let staleIdxs = indexesNeedingLayoutRefresh(at: width)
641	        // Empty → fully cached at this width. Common case when resize
642	        // ended at the start width with no actual change. No push
643	        // happened, so no pop needed.
644	        guard !staleIdxs.isEmpty else { return }
645	
646	        let snapshot = staleIdxs.map { blocks[$0] }
647	        let snapshotCounter = mutationCounter
648	        let tokensSnapshot = highlightStorage.snapshot()
649	        // Push covers the async layout window so the scroller stays hidden
650	        // through the post-resize relayout. Popped via the task's defer in
651	        // every outcome (cancelled, drifted, succeeded).
652	        pushScrollerHidden()
653	
654	        cacheRefillTask?.cancel()
655	        cacheRefillTask = Task.detached(priority: .userInitiated) { [weak self] in
656	            var entries: [(UUID, RowLayout)] = []
657	            entries.reserveCapacity(snapshot.count)
658	            var aborted = false
659	            for block in snapshot {
660	                if Task.isCancelled { aborted = true; break }
661	                entries.append((block.id, Self.makeLayout(
662	                    for: block, width: width, tokens: tokensSnapshot)))
663	            }
664	            await MainActor.run { [entries] in
665	                defer { self?.popScrollerHidden() }
666	                if aborted { return }
667	                guard let self, let table = self.tableView,
668	                      self.layoutWidth == width else { return }
669	                // mutationCounter drift → an `apply` ran during the task.
670	                // Skip the entire onMain (cache writes, noteHeightOfRows,
671	                // saveVisible). Reason: noteHeightOfRows is deferred to
672	                // the next layout pass, so `applyAnchor` would run
673	                // against AppKit's still-stale internal heights and
674	                // produce a wrong scroll compensation; the row visually
675	                // jumps when AppKit eventually re-queries. `apply` has
676	                // already settled its own scroll, and `heightOfRow` will
677	                // lazy-fill the layouts as needed.
678	                guard self.mutationCounter == snapshotCounter else { return }
679	                // applyInBackground (fire-and-forget, counter-untracked)
680	                // may have shifted indices. Re-resolve via id so
681	                // noteHeightOfRows targets the current dataSource state.
682	                let idxs = entries.compactMap { (id, _) -> Int? in
683	                    self.blocks.firstIndex { $0.id == id }
684	                }
685	                self.withScrollAdjustment(.saveVisible(.visualTop), in: table) {
686	                    self.cacheLayouts(entries, width: width)
687	                    if !idxs.isEmpty {
688	                        table.noteHeightOfRows(withIndexesChanged: IndexSet(idxs))
689	                    }
690	                }
691	            }
692	        }
693	    }
694	
695	    // MARK: - NSTableViewDataSource / Delegate
696	
697	    func numberOfRows(in tableView: NSTableView) -> Int { blocks.count }
698	
699	    func tableView(_ tableView: NSTableView, heightOfRow row: Int) -> CGFloat {
700	        guard blocks.indices.contains(row) else { return 1 }
701	        let width = layoutWidth
702	        let pad = BlockStyle.blockPadding(for: blocks[row].kind)
703	        return layout(for: blocks[row], width: width).totalHeight
704	            + pad.top + pad.bottom
705	    }
706	
707	    func tableView(_ tableView: NSTableView,
708	                   rowViewForRow row: Int) -> NSTableRowView? {
709	        let id = NSUserInterfaceItemIdentifier("BlockRow")
710	        if let reused = tableView.makeView(withIdentifier: id, owner: self)
711	            as? CenteredRowView
712	        {
713	            return reused
714	        }
715	        let view = CenteredRowView()
716	        view.identifier = id
717	        return view
718	    }
719	
720	    func tableView(_ tableView: NSTableView,
721	                   viewFor tableColumn: NSTableColumn?,
722	                   row: Int) -> NSView? {
723	        guard blocks.indices.contains(row) else { return nil }
724	        let width = layoutWidth
725	        let block = blocks[row]
726	        let cellLayout = layout(for: block, width: width)
727	
728	        let id = NSUserInterfaceItemIdentifier("BlockCell")
729	        let cell: BlockCellView
730	        if let reused = tableView.makeView(withIdentifier: id, owner: self) as? BlockCellView {
731	            cell = reused
732	        } else {
733	            cell = BlockCellView()

735	        }

738	        // Selection is keyed by block id, not by cell instance, so a
739	        // recycled cell scrolling onto a row that already had a selection
740	        // picks up the existing entry here. nil = no highlight.
741	        cell.blockId = block.id
742	        cell.selection = selection.selection(for: block.id)

744	        // on every reuse so a recycled cell doesn't carry a stale
745	        // checkmark onto a different code block.
746	        cell.resetCopiedFeedback()

748	        // chevron mouseDown can hit `requestUserBubbleSheet` without
749	        // scanning the superview chain.
750	        cell.coordinator = self
751	        return cell
752	    }
753	
754	    // MARK: - Selection helpers (consumed by SelectionCoordinator)
755	
756	    /// Block at `row`, or `nil` if out of bounds. Selection-side reads
757	    /// must accept "no block here" because `applyInBackground`'s
758	    /// fire-and-forget hop can shrink `blocks` between the caller's
759	    /// row resolution and this read.
760	    func block(atRow row: Int) -> Block? {
761	        blocks.indices.contains(row) ? blocks[row] : nil
762	    }
763	
764	    /// Block by id. Linear scan — selection paths touch ≤ N blocks, the
765	    /// dict-keyed lookup elsewhere is `layoutCache`'s job, not this one.
766	    func block(forId id: UUID) -> Block? {
767	        blocks.first { $0.id == id }
768	    }
769	
770	    /// Selection-facing API for the block at `row`, or `nil` if the row
771	    /// is non-selectable (image, list). Goes through the lazy
772	    /// `layout(for:width:)` path so a row whose layout was evicted (or
773	    /// not yet computed) lazy-fills its cache entry as a side effect.
774	    func selectionAdapter(atRow row: Int) -> SelectionAdapter? {
775	        guard let block = block(atRow: row) else { return nil }
776	        return layout(for: block, width: layoutWidth).selectionAdapter
777	    }
778	
779	    /// Selection-facing API keyed by block id (used by Cmd+A, copy, and
780	    /// other paths that don't have a row index handy).
781	    func selectionAdapter(forBlockId id: UUID) -> SelectionAdapter? {
782	        guard let block = block(forId: id) else { return nil }
783	        return layout(for: block, width: layoutWidth).selectionAdapter
784	    }
785	
786	    /// Push the current selection state for `blockId` to its visible
787	    /// cell, which triggers `needsDisplay` via the cell's `didSet` if
788	    /// the value actually changed. No-op if the cell isn't currently
789	    /// visible — when it scrolls in, `viewFor` will read the live state
790	    /// from the selection dict.
791	    func markCellNeedsDisplay(blockId: UUID) {
792	        guard let table = tableView,
793	              let row = blocks.firstIndex(where: { $0.id == blockId })
794	        else { return }
795	        guard let cell = table.view(atColumn: 0, row: row, makeIfNecessary: false)
796	            as? BlockCellView
797	        else { return }
798	        cell.selection = selection.selection(for: blockId)
799	    }
800	
801	}

1	import AppKit
2	
3	/// One interactive hot zone in layout-local coords. Drives both cursor
4	/// swap (`resetCursorRects` registers `.pointingHand` over `rect`) and
5	/// click dispatch (`mouseDown` matches the point against `rect` and
6	/// runs `action`). Cell offsets `rect` by its draw origin before either
7	/// use; layouts emit hits in their own coordinate space.
8	struct InteractiveHit: Sendable {
9	    let rect: CGRect
10	    let action: HitAction
11	}
12	
13	/// What clicking an `InteractiveHit` does. Three cases for three
14	/// independent behaviors — kept as a closed enum so the cell's
15	/// dispatch stays an exhaustive switch. Adding a fourth interaction
16	/// (e.g., "expand inline") = add a case here and a switch arm in
17	/// `BlockCellView.mouseDown`.
18	enum HitAction: Sendable {
19	    /// `.link`-attributed run. Cell opens via `NSWorkspace.shared.open`.
20	    case openURL(URL)
21	    /// User-bubble chevron. Cell forwards to
22	    /// `Transcript2Coordinator.requestUserBubbleSheet(id:)` (the
23	    /// block id lives on the cell, not the layout).
24	    case openUserBubbleSheet
25	    /// Code-block copy button. Cell writes the payload to the
26	    /// general pasteboard and triggers its transient checkmark
27	    /// feedback.
28	    case copyText(String)
29	}
30	
31	/// Type-erased dispatch over the per-kind layout primitives. Holds whichever
32	/// `XxxLayout` value the block kind required, and exposes the things
33	/// downstream code asks for:
34	///
35	/// - `totalHeight` (consumed by `NSTableView.heightOfRow`)
36	/// - `measuredWidth` (used by the layout cache to detect width changes)
37	/// - `draw(in:origin:)` (invoked by the cell's `draw(_:)`)
38	/// - `selectionAdapter` / `iBeamRect` / `interactiveHits` (consumed
39	///   by the cell for selection + cursor rects + click dispatch)
40	///
41	/// Add a new layout primitive: add the case here, extend each method.
42	/// The cell view is enum-agnostic — it doesn't switch on cases, only
43	/// calls these uniform APIs.
44	enum RowLayout: @unchecked Sendable {
45	    case text(TextLayout)
46	    case image(ImageLayout)
47	    case list(ListLayout)
48	    case table(TableLayout)
49	    case codeBlock(CodeBlockLayout)
50	    case blockquote(BlockquoteLayout)
51	    case thematicBreak(ThematicBreakLayout)
52	    case userBubble(UserBubbleLayout)
53	
54	    var totalHeight: CGFloat {
55	        switch self {
56	        case .text(let l): return l.totalHeight
57	        case .image(let l): return l.totalHeight
58	        case .list(let l): return l.totalHeight
59	        case .table(let l): return l.totalHeight
60	        case .codeBlock(let l): return l.totalHeight
61	        case .blockquote(let l): return l.totalHeight
62	        case .thematicBreak(let l): return l.totalHeight
63	        case .userBubble(let l): return l.totalHeight
64	        }
65	    }
66	
67	    var measuredWidth: CGFloat {
68	        switch self {
69	        case .text(let l): return l.measuredWidth
70	        case .image(let l): return l.measuredWidth
71	        case .list(let l): return l.measuredWidth
72	        case .table(let l): return l.measuredWidth
73	        case .codeBlock(let l): return l.measuredWidth
74	        case .blockquote(let l): return l.measuredWidth
75	        case .thematicBreak(let l): return l.measuredWidth
76	        case .userBubble(let l): return l.measuredWidth
77	        }
78	    }
79	
80	    /// Opaque chrome that must paint *before* the cell's selection band
81	    /// so the highlight composites on top, under the glyphs. Default is
82	    /// a no-op — only codeblock currently has an opaque card background
83	    /// that would otherwise hide the selection rect drawn by the cell.
84	    func drawBackplate(in ctx: CGContext, origin: CGPoint) {
85	        switch self {
86	        case .codeBlock(let l): l.drawBackplate(in: ctx, origin: origin)
87	        default: break
88	        }
89	    }
90	
91	    func draw(in ctx: CGContext, origin: CGPoint) {
92	        switch self {
93	        case .text(let l): l.draw(in: ctx, origin: origin)
94	        case .image(let l): l.draw(in: ctx, origin: origin)
95	        case .list(let l): l.draw(in: ctx, origin: origin)
96	        case .table(let l): l.draw(in: ctx, origin: origin)
97	        case .codeBlock(let l): l.draw(in: ctx, origin: origin)
98	        case .blockquote(let l): l.draw(in: ctx, origin: origin)
99	        case .thematicBreak(let l): l.draw(in: ctx, origin: origin)
100	        case .userBubble(let l): l.draw(in: ctx, origin: origin)
101	        }
102	    }
103	
104	    /// Region that should show the I-beam cursor on hover, in
105	    /// layout-local coords. `nil` means "the whole cell `bounds`" —
106	    /// matches `NSTextView`'s behavior of I-beam over the full frame
107	    /// for ordinary text blocks. Right-aligned / non-full-width
108	    /// layouts (currently: user bubble) override to confine the
109	    /// I-beam to their actual content rect, so empty gutter space
110	    /// outside the bubble keeps the default arrow.
111	    var iBeamRect: CGRect? {
112	        switch self {
113	        case .userBubble(let l): return l.bubbleRect
114	        default: return nil
115	        }
116	    }
117	
118	    /// Interactive hot zones in layout-local coords — links + cell-
119	    /// internal controls (user bubble chevron, code-block copy
120	    /// button), unified as `(rect, action)`. The cell iterates this
121	    /// list once for `resetCursorRects` (every entry → pointing-
122	    /// hand) and once for `mouseDown` (first hit wins; switch on
123	    /// `action`). List / table / blockquote layouts have already
124	    /// flattened their inner-cell links into container-local coords
125	    /// during `make`, so they roll up here without further transform.
126	    var interactiveHits: [InteractiveHit] {
127	        var hits: [InteractiveHit] = []
128	        let links: [TextLayout.LinkHit]
129	        switch self {
130	        case .text(let l): links = l.links
131	        case .list(let l): links = l.links
132	        case .table(let l): links = l.links
133	        case .codeBlock(let l): links = l.links
134	        case .blockquote(let l): links = l.links
135	        case .image, .thematicBreak, .userBubble: links = []
136	        }
137	        hits.append(contentsOf: links.map {
138	            InteractiveHit(rect: $0.rect, action: .openURL($0.url))
139	        })
140	        switch self {
141	        case .userBubble(let l):
142	            if let r = l.chevronHitRect {
143	                hits.append(InteractiveHit(rect: r, action: .openUserBubbleSheet))
144	            }
145	        case .codeBlock(let l):
146	            if let r = l.copyHitRect {
147	                hits.append(InteractiveHit(rect: r, action: .copyText(l.code)))
148	            }
149	        default:
150	            break
151	        }
152	        return hits
153	    }
154	
155	    /// Selection-facing API for this row, or `nil` for non-selectable
156	    /// kinds (image, thematic break). The selection coordinator and cell
157	    /// view consume only this — the underlying `TextLayout` /
158	    /// `TableLayout` / `ListLayout` type is encapsulated, so adding a new
159	    /// selectable kind needs no changes outside the new layout's own
160	    /// file.
161	    var selectionAdapter: SelectionAdapter? {
162	        switch self {
163	        case .text(let l): return l.selectionAdapter
164	        case .table(let l): return l.selectionAdapter
165	        case .list(let l): return l.selectionAdapter
166	        case .codeBlock(let l): return l.selectionAdapter
167	        case .blockquote(let l): return l.selectionAdapter
168	        case .image: return nil
169	        case .thematicBreak: return nil
170	        case .userBubble(let l): return l.selectionAdapter
171	        }
172	    }
173	}

1	import AppKit
2	
3	/// Custom-drawn row cell. Holds a prepared `RowLayout`, draws it via
4	/// `override draw(_:)`.
5	///
6	/// Layer policy: `wantsLayer = true` + `.onSetNeedsDisplay`. The cached
7	/// layer bitmap is reused during scroll (zero `draw(_:)` calls), and AppKit
8	/// only re-issues `draw(_:)` after we mark `needsDisplay = true`.
9	///
10	/// ### Cursor + click dispatch
11	///
12	/// The cell is layout-agnostic for both. `RowLayout` exposes:
13	/// - `iBeamRect`: where the I-beam should show on hover (`nil` = full
14	///   cell `bounds`, the default for full-width text blocks; user-bubble
15	///   confines this to its right-aligned `bubbleRect` so the empty left
16	///   gutter keeps the default arrow).
17	/// - `interactiveHits`: list of `(rect, HitAction)` pairs covering URL
18	///   links, the user-bubble chevron, and the code-block copy button.
19	///
20	/// `resetCursorRects` registers I-beam over `iBeamRect` and pointing-
21	/// hand over each `interactiveHits` rect. AppKit handles hover cursor
22	/// swap automatically — no tracking area needed.
23	///
24	/// `mouseDown` walks the same `interactiveHits` list once, switches on
25	/// the matched `HitAction`, and falls through to selection-drag
26	/// forwarding when no hit is found. Adding a new in-cell control means
27	/// emitting another `InteractiveHit` from the relevant layout and
28	/// adding one switch arm here — no new `if case .xxx = layout` chains.
29	///
30	/// ### Selection
31	///
32	/// `selectedRange` is set by `Transcript2Coordinator` (read from
33	/// `Transcript2SelectionCoordinator`). Length-0 means no selection. The
34	/// highlight paints **before** the glyphs in `draw(_:)`, matching
35	/// `NSTextView`'s order so anti-aliased glyphs blend correctly against
36	/// the system selection background.
37	///
38	/// Color: `selectedTextBackgroundColor` (key window) /
39	/// `unemphasizedSelectedTextBackgroundColor` (resigned). Both are dynamic
40	/// colors that auto-resolve to the cell's effective appearance and the
41	/// system Accent Color, so we never construct a custom selection color.
42	///
43	/// `mouseDown` not consumed by a link is forwarded directly to the
44	/// enclosing `NSTableView` so its tracking-loop override can run text
45	/// selection. Using `nextResponder?.mouseDown` would route through
46	/// `NSTableRowView`, whose default mouseDown forwarding behavior is not
47	/// guaranteed; the explicit walk is deterministic.
48	final class BlockCellView: NSView {
49	    var layout: RowLayout? {
50	        didSet {
51	            needsDisplay = true
52	            // Cursor rects are cached by AppKit per-view; layout changes
53	            // (resize, content swap) invalidate those caches so the new
54	            // link rects take effect on the next mouse motion.
55	            window?.invalidateCursorRects(for: self)
56	        }
57	    }
58	
59	    /// Set by `viewFor` so selection-driven repaints (window-key flip,
60	    /// drag updates) can be addressed back to this cell. Stale on a
61	    /// recycled cell until the next `viewFor` runs — that's fine because
62	    /// recycle-driven repaint goes through the same `viewFor`.
63	    var blockId: UUID?
64	
65	    /// Set by `viewFor`. Used by mouseDown when a hit lands on a control
66	    /// belonging to the cell's layout (currently: user bubble chevron).
67	    /// Selection drag still walks to the enclosing `NSTableView` because
68	    /// AppKit's tracking loop owns that gesture — only cell-internal
69	    /// controls go through this reference.
70	    weak var coordinator: Transcript2Coordinator?
71	
72	    /// Top padding contributed by the block's row (per-kind via
73	    /// `BlockStyle.blockPadding(for:)`). Drives `layoutOrigin.y` and
74	    /// selection rect offsetting. Set by `viewFor` alongside `layout`.
75	    var padTop: CGFloat = 0
76	
77	    /// Current selection for this cell's block. `nil` = no selection.
78	    /// `didSet` triggers `needsDisplay` when the value actually changes;
79	    /// identical assignments are absorbed.
80	    var selection: SelectionRange? {
81	        didSet {
82	            if selection != oldValue {
83	                needsDisplay = true
84	            }
85	        }
86	    }
87	
88	    /// Timestamp of the most recent copy click on this cell's code
89	    /// block, or `nil` when the button should display its idle
90	    /// (`doc.on.doc`) glyph. Set on click; cleared 1.5s later by a
91	    /// task that compares the timestamp before clearing (so a quick
92	    /// second click doesn't get its checkmark cut short by the first
93	    /// click's pending clear). Reset to `nil` on every `viewFor`
94	    /// reuse — the feedback is opportunistic, missing it on a
95	    /// scroll-recycled cell is fine.
96	    private var copiedAt: Date?
97	
98	    /// Public reset hook for `viewFor` so a recycled cell never shows
99	    /// a stale checkmark on a different block.
100	    func resetCopiedFeedback() {
101	        if copiedAt != nil {
102	            copiedAt = nil
103	            needsDisplay = true
104	        }
105	    }
106	
107	    override var isFlipped: Bool { true }
108	    override var wantsDefaultClipping: Bool { false }
109	
110	    override init(frame frameRect: NSRect) {
111	        super.init(frame: frameRect)
112	        wantsLayer = true
113	        layerContentsRedrawPolicy = .onSetNeedsDisplay
114	    }
115	
116	    required init?(coder: NSCoder) { fatalError() }
117	
118	    private var layoutOrigin: CGPoint {
119	        CGPoint(
120	            x: BlockStyle.blockHorizontalPadding,
121	            y: padTop)
122	    }
123	
124	    override func draw(_ dirtyRect: NSRect) {
125	        guard let layout, let ctx = NSGraphicsContext.current?.cgContext else { return }
126	        let origin = layoutOrigin
127	
128	        // Backplate: opaque chrome that must paint *before* the
129	        // selection band so the highlight composites on top of (not
130	        // under) the card. No-op for everything except codeblock,
131	        // which has an opaque editor-canvas fill.
132	        layout.drawBackplate(in: ctx, origin: origin)
133	
134	        // Selection highlight: under glyphs, matching NSTextView ordering.
135	        // The adapter projects (start, end) → layout-local rects; what
136	        // those rects mean (text glyph band / cell rectangle / 1×1 inner
137	        // band) is fully encapsulated inside the layout's adapter.
138	        if let selection, let adapter = layout.selectionAdapter {
139	            let rects = adapter.rects(selection.start, selection.end)
140	            if !rects.isEmpty {
141	                let color: NSColor = (window?.isKeyWindow == true)
142	                    ? .selectedTextBackgroundColor
143	                    : .unemphasizedSelectedTextBackgroundColor
144	                ctx.setFillColor(color.cgColor)
145	                for rect in rects {
146	                    // `integral` keeps the bg edges crisp on Retina at
147	                    // non-1× scale.
148	                    ctx.fill(rect.offsetBy(dx: origin.x, dy: origin.y).integral)
149	                }
150	            }
151	        }
152	
153	        layout.draw(in: ctx, origin: origin)
154	
155	        // Code-block copy glyph — layout owns the visual recipe
156	        // (symbol, tint, size); the cell only owns the trigger and
157	        // hands its transient `copiedAt` flag through as `checked`.
158	        if case .codeBlock(let l) = layout {
159	            l.drawCopyGlyph(in: ctx, origin: origin, checked: copiedAt != nil)
160	        }
161	    }
162	
163	    // MARK: - Link interaction
164	
165	    override func resetCursorRects() {
166	        super.resetCursorRects()
167	        guard let layout else { return }
168	        let origin = layoutOrigin
169	        // I-beam: over `iBeamRect` if the layout confines it
170	        // (user bubble), else the whole cell `bounds` to match
171	        // `NSTextView`'s "I-beam over full frame" behavior. Order
172	        // matters: when cursor rects overlap, the most-recently-
173	        // added wins, so pointing-hand rects registered below take
174	        // priority over the I-beam in their hot zones. Non-
175	        // selectable rows (image, thematic break) skip — they get
176	        // the default arrow.
177	        if layout.selectionAdapter != nil {
178	            let rect = layout.iBeamRect.map {
179	                $0.offsetBy(dx: origin.x, dy: origin.y)
180	            } ?? bounds
181	            addCursorRect(rect, cursor: .iBeam)
182	        }
183	        for hit in layout.interactiveHits {
184	            addCursorRect(hit.rect.offsetBy(dx: origin.x, dy: origin.y),
185	                          cursor: .pointingHand)
186	        }
187	    }
188	
189	    override func mouseDown(with event: NSEvent) {
190	        if let action = hitAction(at: event) {
191	            switch action {
192	            case .openURL(let url):
193	                NSWorkspace.shared.open(url)
194	            case .openUserBubbleSheet:
195	                // The only well-defined exit point from AppKit-internal
196	                // interactions to SwiftUI, since `.sheet(item:)` lives
197	                // on the SwiftUI side. Selection-drag continues to stay
198	                // inside `Transcript2SelectionCoordinator`.
199	                if let id = blockId {
200	                    coordinator?.requestUserBubbleSheet(id: id)
201	                }
202	            case .copyText(let text):
203	                copyToPasteboard(text)
204	            }
205	            return
206	        }
207	        // Forward to the enclosing table so its tracking loop owns the
208	        // gesture (text-selection drag). Walk the superview chain rather
209	        // than relying on `nextResponder` — `NSTableRowView`'s default
210	        // mouseDown behavior isn't documented as forwarding.
211	        var v: NSView? = superview
212	        while let cur = v {
213	            if let table = cur as? NSTableView {
214	                table.mouseDown(with: event)
215	                return
216	            }
217	            v = cur.superview
218	        }
219	    }
220	
221	    private func hitAction(at event: NSEvent) -> HitAction? {
222	        guard let layout else { return nil }
223	        let local = convert(event.locationInWindow, from: nil)
224	        let origin = layoutOrigin
225	        for hit in layout.interactiveHits {
226	            if hit.rect.offsetBy(dx: origin.x, dy: origin.y).contains(local) {
227	                return hit.action
228	            }
229	        }
230	        return nil
231	    }
232	
233	    private func copyToPasteboard(_ text: String) {
234	        let pb = NSPasteboard.general
235	        pb.clearContents()
236	        pb.setString(text, forType: .string)
237	
238	        // Visual feedback: swap idle → checkmark, schedule a swap back
239	        // 1.5s later. Stamp identity prevents a quick second click's
240	        // checkmark from being cut short by the first click's
241	        // pending clear.
242	        let stamp = Date()
243	        copiedAt = stamp
244	        needsDisplay = true
245	        Task { @MainActor [weak self] in
246	            try? await Task.sleep(nanoseconds: 1_500_000_000)
247	            guard let self, self.copiedAt == stamp else { return }
248	            self.copiedAt = nil
249	            self.needsDisplay = true
250	        }
251	    }
252	}

1	import AgentSDK
2	import AppKit
3	import CoreText
4	import QuartzCore
5	
6	/// 连续 tool_use 聚合 row —— 不论 tool kind,所有 assistant 消息只要 content
7	/// 全是 tool_use 就归这里。Header(title + chevron + shimmer)+ 展开态内嵌
8	/// N 条 child(per-tool dispatch 到 ``GroupChildRenderer``:Read 是 header-style
9	/// 文字行,其它是虚线占位框)。
10	///
11	/// `isActive = (entryIndex == entryCount - 1)` —— group 是 `entries.last` 即
12	/// 视作运行中。
13	///
14	/// 整 group(header + 所有 child)= **一个 NSTableView row**。toggle 展开
15	/// 走 `applyState` 的 `relayouted` 快路径,无 row insert/delete。子项**不**
16	/// 实现 `TranscriptComponent`,而是 ``GroupChildRenderer`` plug-in。
17	enum GroupComponent: TranscriptComponent {
18	    static let tag = "Group"
19	
20	    // MARK: - Input / Content / Layout / State
21	
22	    struct Input: @unchecked Sendable {
23	        let stableId: StableId
24	        /// 折叠态 active title —— 最后一条 tool 的进行时短语。
25	        let activeBriefTitle: String
26	        /// 展开态 active title —— 聚合进行时短语(`Reading 3 files · …`)。
27	        let activeAggregatedTitle: String
28	        /// 完成态 title —— 聚合过去时短语(`Read 3 files · …`)。
29	        let completedTitle: String
30	        let isActive: Bool
31	        /// 全部 tool_use,按 group item 顺序展平(单个 item 可能包含多个 tool_use)。
32	        let toolUses: [ToolUseEntry]
33	
34	        struct ToolUseEntry: @unchecked Sendable {
35	            let toolUseId: String
36	            let tool: ToolUse
37	        }
38	    }
39	
40	    struct Content: @unchecked Sendable {
41	        let isActive: Bool
42	        /// 三套 title 各自 prepare 一次 —— 切换不重 CT measure。
43	        let activeBrief: TitleMeasure
44	        let activeAggregated: TitleMeasure
45	        let completed: TitleMeasure
46	        /// per-child parsed payload,顺序 = ``Input.toolUses``。

51	            let content: GroupChildContent

65	            case (true, false):  return activeBrief

73	        let content: Content

78	        let hitRect: CGRect

103	    // MARK: - Inputs
104	
105	    nonisolated static func inputs(
106	        from entry: MessageEntry,
107	        entryIndex: Int,
108	        entryCount: Int
109	    ) -> [IdentifiedInput<Input>] {
110	        guard case .group(let group) = entry else { return [] }
111	        let isActive = (entryIndex == entryCount - 1)
112	        let stableId = StableId(entryId: group.id, locator: .whole)
113	
114	        // 把整 group 的所有 tool_use 按 (item, blockOrder) 顺序展平。`tool.id` 在
115	        // generated 类型里是 `String?` —— 缺失场景(jsonl fixture 偶发) 用 toolUseId
116	        // 当兜底,group children 的相对顺序仍由展平顺序决定。

120	                toolUses.append(.init(toolUseId: tool.id ?? "", tool: tool))
121	            }
122	        }
123	
124	        return [IdentifiedInput(
125	            stableId: stableId,
126	            entryIndex: entryIndex,
127	            blockIndex: 0,
128	            input: Input(
129	                stableId: stableId,
130	                activeBriefTitle: group.activeTitle,
131	                activeAggregatedTitle: group.expandedActiveTitle,
132	                completedTitle: group.completedTitle,
133	                isActive: isActive,
134	                toolUses: toolUses))]
135	    }

137	    // MARK: - Prepare
138	
139	    nonisolated static func prepare(
140	        _ input: Input,
141	        theme: TranscriptTheme
142	    ) -> Content {
143	        let active = measure(input.activeBriefTitle, theme: theme)
144	        let activeAgg = measure(input.activeAggregatedTitle, theme: theme)
145	        let completed = measure(input.completedTitle, theme: theme)
146	        let children = input.toolUses.map { entry in
147	            Content.ChildEntry(
148	                toolUseId: entry.toolUseId,
149	                content: GroupChildDispatch.parse(entry.tool, theme: theme))
150	        }
151	        return Content(
152	            isActive: input.isActive,
153	            activeBrief: active,
154	            activeAggregated: activeAgg,
155	            completed: completed,
156	            children: children)
157	    }
158	
159	    nonisolated private static func measure(
160	        _ text: String, theme: TranscriptTheme
161	    ) -> Content.TitleMeasure {
162	        let attrs: [NSAttributedString.Key: Any] = [.font: theme.groupTitleFont]
163	        let str = NSAttributedString(string: text, attributes: attrs)
164	        let line = CTLineCreateWithAttributedString(str)
165	        var ascent: CGFloat = 0, descent: CGFloat = 0, leading: CGFloat = 0
166	        let width = CTLineGetTypographicBounds(line, &ascent, &descent, &leading)
167	        return Content.TitleMeasure(
168	            text: text,
169	            width: CGFloat(width),
170	            ascent: ascent,
171	            descent: descent)
172	    }
173	
174	    nonisolated static func contentHash(
175	        _ input: Input,
176	        theme: TranscriptTheme
177	    ) -> Int {
178	        var h = Hasher()
179	        h.combine(input.activeBriefTitle)
180	        h.combine(input.activeAggregatedTitle)
181	        h.combine(input.completedTitle)
182	        h.combine(input.isActive)
183	        h.combine(input.toolUses.count)
184	        for entry in input.toolUses {
185	            h.combine(entry.toolUseId)
186	            h.combine(GroupChildDispatch.contentHash(entry.tool, theme: theme))
187	        }
188	        h.combine(theme.markdown.fingerprint)
189	        return h.finalize()
190	    }
191	
192	    nonisolated static func initialState(for input: Input) -> State {
193	        .default
194	    }
195	
196	    // MARK: - Layout
197	
198	    nonisolated static func layout(
199	        _ content: Content,
200	        theme: TranscriptTheme,
201	        width: CGFloat,
202	        state: State
203	    ) -> Layout {
204	        geometry(content: content, theme: theme, width: width, state: state)
205	    }
206	
207	    /// 快路径:width + content 未变,只 `isExpanded` 翻转 → 重选 title +
208	    /// 重算 childFrames + cachedHeight。
209	    nonisolated static func relayouted(
210	        _ layout: Layout,
211	        theme: TranscriptTheme,
212	        state: State
213	    ) -> Layout? {
214	        guard layout.laidOutExpanded != state.isExpanded else { return layout }
215	        return geometry(
216	            content: layout.content,
217	            theme: theme,
218	            width: layout.cachedWidth,
219	            state: state)
220	    }
221	
222	    nonisolated private static func geometry(
223	        content: Content,
224	        theme: TranscriptTheme,
225	        width: CGFloat,
226	        state: State
227	    ) -> Layout {
228	        let hPad = theme.rowHorizontalPadding
229	        let headerTop = theme.rowVerticalPadding
230	        let headerH = theme.groupHeaderHeight
231	        let rowContentWidth = max(0, width - 2 * hPad)
232	        let chevronSize = theme.groupChevronDrawSize
233	        let gap = theme.groupChevronGap
234	
235	        let title = content.pickTitle(isExpanded: state.isExpanded)
236	
237	        // Title 占据文字宽度,但给 chevron + gap 留空间。
238	        let reservedForChevron = chevronSize + gap
239	        let titleMaxWidth = max(0, rowContentWidth - reservedForChevron)
240	        let titleWidth = min(title.width, titleMaxWidth)
241	
242	        let titleH = title.ascent + title.descent
243	        let headerMidY = headerTop + headerH / 2
244	        let titleRect = CGRect(
245	            x: hPad,
246	            y: headerMidY - titleH / 2,
247	            width: titleWidth,
248	            height: titleH)
249	
250	        // Chevron 视觉居中补偿 —— 同原实现。
251	        let font = theme.groupTitleFont
252	        let visualCompensation = max(0, (font.capHeight - font.xHeight) / 2)
253	        let chevronRect = CGRect(
254	            x: titleRect.maxX + gap,
255	            y: headerMidY - chevronSize / 2 + visualCompensation,
256	            width: chevronSize,
257	            height: chevronSize)
258	
259	        let hitPad = theme.groupHitPadding
260	        let hitMinX = max(hPad, titleRect.minX - hitPad)
261	        let hitMaxX = min(hPad + rowContentWidth, chevronRect.maxX + hitPad)
262	        let hitRect = CGRect(
263	            x: hitMinX,
264	            y: headerTop,
265	            width: max(0, hitMaxX - hitMinX),
266	            height: headerH)
267	
268	        // Children:gestalt 接近性下,header → first child 间距 = child ↔ child
269	        // 间距 = 4pt(``groupChildSpacing``)。最后一个 child 后接 row 自身的
270	        // bottom rowVerticalPadding,继而和下条 entry 的 top padding 加和成 ≥ 24pt
271	        // 视觉分隔(l0)。
272	        var childFrames: [GroupChildFrame] = []
273	        var totalHeight = headerTop + headerH
274	        if state.isExpanded && !content.children.isEmpty {
275	            var y = totalHeight + theme.groupChildSpacing
276	            for child in content.children {
277	                let frame = GroupChildDispatch.layout(
278	                    child.content,
279	                    x: hPad,
280	                    y: y,
281	                    width: rowContentWidth,
282	                    theme: theme)
283	                let h = GroupChildDispatch.height(frame)
284	                childFrames.append(frame)
285	                y += h + theme.groupChildSpacing
286	            }
287	            // 移除最后一次多加的 spacing(child 后面紧接 row vertical padding)。
288	            y -= theme.groupChildSpacing
289	            totalHeight = y
290	        }
291	        totalHeight += theme.rowVerticalPadding
292	
293	        return Layout(
294	            content: content,
295	            titleText: title.text,
296	            titleRect: titleRect,
297	            chevronRect: chevronRect,
298	            hitRect: hitRect,
299	            childFrames: childFrames,
300	            cachedHeight: totalHeight,
301	            cachedWidth: width,
302	            laidOutExpanded: state.isExpanded)
303	    }
304	
305	    // MARK: - Render
306	
307	    @MainActor
308	    static func render(
309	        _ layout: Layout,
310	        state: State,
311	        theme: TranscriptTheme,
312	        sideCar: GroupSideCar,
313	        in ctx: CGContext,
314	        bounds: CGRect
315	    ) {
316	        // CGContext 路径只画 children;header(title / chevron / shimmer)由
317	        // SideCar 的 CALayer 完成。
318	        for (frame, child) in zip(layout.childFrames, layout.content.children) {
319	            GroupChildDispatch.draw(child.content, frame: frame, theme: theme, in: ctx)
320	        }
321	
322	        sideCar.sync(
323	            title: layout.titleText,
324	            titleRect: layout.titleRect,
325	            chevronRect: layout.chevronRect,
326	            isActive: layout.content.isActive,
327	            isExpanded: state.isExpanded,
328	            theme: theme)
329	    }
330	
331	    // MARK: - SideCar factory
332	
333	    @MainActor
334	    static func makeSideCar(for content: Content) -> GroupSideCar {
335	        GroupSideCar()
336	    }
337	
338	    // MARK: - Interactions
339	
340	    @MainActor
341	    static func interactions(
342	        _ layout: Layout,
343	        state: State
344	    ) -> [Interaction<Self>] {
345	        let hit = layout.hitRect
346	        return [
347	            .custom(
348	                rect: hit,
349	                cursor: .pointingHand,
350	                handler: { ctx in
351	                    // 把 row-height 平滑插值 + chevron 旋转 + title fade 全部
352	                    // 包在同一个 NSAnimationContext 里 —— NSTableView 在
353	                    // `noteHeightOfRows` 时按当前 group 的 duration 走 builtin
354	                    // CABasicAnimation(高度从旧 cachedHeight 平滑到新值)。
355	                    NSAnimationContext.runAnimationGroup { animCtx in
356	                        animCtx.duration = ctx.theme.groupExpandDuration
357	                        animCtx.timingFunction = CAMediaTimingFunction(name: .easeInEaseOut)
358	                        var newState = ctx.currentState()
359	                        newState.isExpanded.toggle()
360	                        ctx.applyState(newState, animated: true)
361	                    }
362	                }),
363	            .hover(
364	                rect: hit,
365	                cursor: .pointingHand,
366	                onEnter: { ctx in ctx.sideCar().setHovered(true, theme: ctx.theme) },
367	                onExit:  { ctx in ctx.sideCar().setHovered(false, theme: ctx.theme) }),
368	        ]
369	    }
370	}

1	import AgentSDK
2	import AppKit
3	import CoreText
4	
5	// MARK: - GroupChildRenderer protocol
6	//
7	// 每个 GroupChild 的 parse / layout / draw 实现 —— 整 group 才是一个 NSTableView
8	// row,child 只是 GroupComponent.Content / Layout / render 内部的子元素,**不**
9	// 实现 `TranscriptComponent`。
10	//
11	// 加新富化 tool = 新建一个 struct conform to GroupChildRenderer + 在
12	// `GroupChildContent` / `GroupChildFrame` 加 case + 在 `dispatch(_:)` 加 switch
13	// 分支。没有 SideCar、cache key、stableId 这些 row-level concerns。
14	
15	protocol GroupChildRenderer {
16	    associatedtype Content: Sendable
17	    associatedtype Frame: Sendable
18	
19	    static func parse(_ tool: ToolUse, theme: TranscriptTheme) -> Content
20	    static func contentHash(_ tool: ToolUse, theme: TranscriptTheme) -> Int
21	
22	    /// `(x, y)` row-local 起点;width 是内容列宽度。
23	    static func layout(
24	        _ content: Content,
25	        x: CGFloat,
26	        y: CGFloat,
27	        width: CGFloat,
28	        theme: TranscriptTheme
29	    ) -> Frame
30	
31	    static func height(_ frame: Frame) -> CGFloat
32	
33	    @MainActor
34	    static func draw(
35	        _ content: Content,
36	        frame: Frame,
37	        theme: TranscriptTheme,
38	        in ctx: CGContext
39	    )
40	}
41	
42	// MARK: - Discriminated union (dispatch storage)
43	//
44	// `GroupComponent.Content.children` 直接持 `[GroupChildContent]`,layout 阶段产出
45	// `[GroupChildFrame]`。union 的 case 即等价于 dispatch 表 —— 加新 renderer 时
46	// 在这两个 enum 各加一 case + 在下方 `dispatch` switch 加分支。
47	
48	enum GroupChildContent: @unchecked Sendable {
49	    case read(ReadChildRenderer.Content)
50	    case placeholder(PlaceholderChildRenderer.Content)
51	}
52	
53	enum GroupChildFrame: Sendable {
54	    case read(ReadChildRenderer.Frame)
55	    case placeholder(PlaceholderChildRenderer.Frame)
56	}
57	
58	// MARK: - Dispatch helpers
59	
60	enum GroupChildDispatch {
61	
62	    /// 按 ToolUse 类型选择 renderer 并 parse 出 Content。
63	    nonisolated static func parse(_ tool: ToolUse, theme: TranscriptTheme) -> GroupChildContent {
64	        switch tool {
65	        case .Read:
66	            return .read(ReadChildRenderer.parse(tool, theme: theme))
67	        default:
68	            return .placeholder(PlaceholderChildRenderer.parse(tool, theme: theme))
69	        }
70	    }
71	
72	    nonisolated static func contentHash(_ tool: ToolUse, theme: TranscriptTheme) -> Int {
73	        switch tool {
74	        case .Read:
75	            return ReadChildRenderer.contentHash(tool, theme: theme)
76	        default:
77	            return PlaceholderChildRenderer.contentHash(tool, theme: theme)
78	        }
79	    }
80	
81	    nonisolated static func layout(
82	        _ content: GroupChildContent,
83	        x: CGFloat,
84	        y: CGFloat,
85	        width: CGFloat,
86	        theme: TranscriptTheme
87	    ) -> GroupChildFrame {
88	        switch content {
89	        case .read(let c):
90	            return .read(ReadChildRenderer.layout(c, x: x, y: y, width: width, theme: theme))
91	        case .placeholder(let c):
92	            return .placeholder(PlaceholderChildRenderer.layout(c, x: x, y: y, width: width, theme: theme))
93	        }
94	    }
95	
96	    nonisolated static func height(_ frame: GroupChildFrame) -> CGFloat {
97	        switch frame {
98	        case .read(let f):        return ReadChildRenderer.height(f)
99	        case .placeholder(let f): return PlaceholderChildRenderer.height(f)
100	        }
101	    }
102	
103	    @MainActor
104	    static func draw(
105	        _ content: GroupChildContent,
106	        frame: GroupChildFrame,
107	        theme: TranscriptTheme,
108	        in ctx: CGContext
109	    ) {
110	        switch (content, frame) {
111	        case (.read(let c), .read(let f)):
112	            ReadChildRenderer.draw(c, frame: f, theme: theme, in: ctx)
113	        case (.placeholder(let c), .placeholder(let f)):
114	            PlaceholderChildRenderer.draw(c, frame: f, theme: theme, in: ctx)
115	        default:
116	            // Content / Frame 必须同 case —— layout 由 parse 结果驱动,不可能错配。
117	            assertionFailure("GroupChildDispatch.draw: content/frame kind mismatch")
118	        }
119	    }
120	}
